teaching_language: English; study track held completely in English of the master's degree programme in Strategie di comunicazione.

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year bachelor's degree (or equivalent) in Communication or an equivalent field; country-based entry title and GPA requirements also apply.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Communication or equivalent.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: The official page requires a degree in Communication or an equivalent field, but it does not publish a detailed subject-credit breakdown for the English track [uncertain].
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Applicants must verify country-based specific entry title and GPA requirements through the University of Padua admissions system; the programme page does not publish a programme-specific GPA threshold [uncertain].

accepted_proofs: An accepted English language certificate with the minimum score required for degree programmes and study tracks held in English.
A bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification delivered entirely in English, provided the language of instruction is explicitly documented.
Native-speaker status proved by an identity document showing an eligible English-speaking nationality.
exemption_rules: Applicants who hold a degree delivered entirely in English can use that qualification to satisfy the language requirement.
Applicants considered native speakers of English under the university's nationality list can satisfy the requirement through an identity document.
Candidates who have taken a recognised test but have not yet received the certificate may submit the provisional statement of results issued by the certifying body.
language_requirements.deadline: The central university page states that deadlines are set by the relevant call for admissions; no separate programme-specific language-proof deadline is stated on the Communication Strategies page [uncertain].

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not available to non-EU students residing outside Italy; Call Three is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The official programme page explicitly limits Call Three to EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy. Call Two is listed without a residency restriction on the programme page.

required_documents.notes: Required for master's degree applicants with an international entry qualification.
The central application page lists motivational letter(s) in English for master's applications.
Upload the transcript at application stage; if it is not issued in English or Italian, official translations are required.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree diploma or enrolment/provisional certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
The central page specifies an ID card for EU or equivalent applicants and a passport for others.
Required only for non-EU citizens already residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or equivalent proof
required_documents.notes: Required where the programme asks for English proof; Communication Strategies requires English at B2 level or equivalent.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into English or Italian for academic documents issued in another language
required_documents.notes: The programme page states that official translations are needed when the transcript is not available in English or Italian.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: Listed under the master's degree enrolment documents page.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalised
required_documents.notes: The enrolment page requires a translated and legalised transcript stating exams, grades, and programme description.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through the Universitaly portal no later than 15 July.
Required for non-EU applicants at enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No programme-specific entry test is stated on the official pages [uncertain]
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Admission is based on degree and curricular requirements rather than a stated written test or interview [uncertain].

source_quotes.quote: Access Open access with admission requirements
A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Communication or equivalent
study track - held completely in English

uncertainty_notes: The task-supplied Italian programme URL with ordinamento 2020 redirected to the university homepage on 2026-06-03, so the current 2025 educational-offer page and the 2026/27 apply.unipd.it course page were used instead.
The official programme page for the English track does not publish a detailed subject-credit breakdown or a programme-specific GPA threshold.
No separate programme-specific admission notice PDF was located; the official apply.unipd.it course page was used as the admission-call source.
